description: Extract proof terms via a custom Lean InfoTree extractor (have-trees, premises, proof terms) over frozen Mathlib, round-trip each pretty-printed term-mode proof back through Lean (example := <term>) with an axiom audit, then join verified rough terms with the original tactic source into (rough, fine) pairs.
motivation: Semantics preservation must be machine-checked, not assumed, or the corpus is untrustworthy.
formal_results: 3,151 extracted proof terms; 2,668 (84.7%) round-trip; filtering to tactic-block decls yields 607 verified (rough, fine) pairs across 20 modules (top: Algebra.Group.Basic 137, Analysis.Normed.Group.Basic 82, Data.List.Basic 64). The 15.3% loss is a structural ceiling (private _proof_N/match_N helpers with no parseable source alias).
results_description: A 607-pair verified corpus of the same theorems at rough and fine granularity.
conclusions: Verified reconstruction works at scale (84.7%); the corpus is real and machine-checked, with a known structural ceiling.
